reviews.review_text: It's a great part of Tomb Raider; the optimization is just fantastic. If you like games with a linear storyline, I highly recommend it.

i think this would’ve been the best game in the series if the developers didn’t drag out the ending so much.

the first half of the game was amazing - same beautiful level design, much better and more interesting puzzles and tombs, and a lot of actually enjoyable side quests (i usually hate side quests, but here they were pretty good).

but at some point the game just becomes unnecessarily long. and there’s one thing about the replayability that i genuinely don’t understand - while exploring, you’ll keep finding tombs that stay locked UNTIL you get a specific piece of gear later in the game. why?? after finishing the main story i have absolutely no desire to go back through old locations just for tombs. if i could fully explore them naturally during the story, i probably would’ve spent way more time in the game. i get why they did it, i just don’t think it was a good design decision.

the game feels fresher than the 2013 one, but also way more dragged out.

ps. baba yaga dlc 10/10

It is mindless and fun.  You get to go around and shoot people. Granted, I wish they had the voice activation like the other TR game, but alas you can't have everything.  The achievements through expeditions IMO suck.  They are not fun. The achievements should solely be based on the game IMO and not forced a stupid retarded expedition of collect floating ♥♥♥♥ or forced co op.
even years later, this game still holds up incredibly well with its perfect balance of story, exploration, and action.

Really decent and solid adventure + action game. Although there is combat and action set-pieces similar to something like Uncharted, surprisingly a majority of the game is exploring, scouring an area for resources, and light platforming. Though the main path forward is somewhat linear, the entire map (minus the prologue area until after the end-game) is available to be explored for the optional content.

The optional content isn't just collectibles like your standard relics either: it can be entire tombs (essentially like a mini-dungeon, with one or two main puzzles to be solved), crypts (essentially like a mini tomb), and artifacts that boost your stats. A lot of thought has been put into the optional exploration, especially in the case of the language stat directly dedicated to exploration. For instance, Lara progressively gets better at understanding languages from reading documents and murals, which eventually allows Lara to read monoliths which can find hidden treasure. 

Pretty much every action, whether it be finding resources, artifacts, or completing tombs, feeds into a positive feedback loop to promote more exploration. All of this also feeds into getting more XP, which you can spend to give buffs to Lara.

There isn't much negative to say about RotTR minus some very minor things. For some reason when playing on a controller, the deadzone and mouse acceleration feels really off. The sensitivity is really low at first, then accelerates like crazy. It's also difficult to make super minor adjustments because of the low sens, even though I've modified my controller settings in Steam to read minor inputs. Also, a part of me wishes there were more setpieces, since they felt pretty sparse when compared to the main content of primarily exploring. (That said, this could be because I was trying to 100% the game. If you skipped past a lot of the optional content and skipped doing a 100% run on a first playthrough, this might not be the case). There are also some minor quirks with the platforming; although mostly very solid, if Lara as so much steps on a piece of geometry she isn't supposed to, she gets yeeted downwards, which could potentially get you killed. (Otherwise the platforming is solid and Lara is very controllable. Although, maybe too controllable, since Lara can defy physics and change her trajectory completely and do a 180 mid-air). I guess I also wish some of the hub areas were more expansive like the Geothermal Valley area, which I think is the best area in the game.

In some ways, having more of an emphasis on exploring rather than the setpieces makes it feel more oldschool; so if you like that, then it's totally worth checking this game out.

There's also additional content in the form of optional non-story missions you can play in the main menu. These are pretty decent, with the Croft Manor ones being the most fleshed out. The Croft Manor mission puts a 100% emphasis on exploring and puzzle solving (with a bunch of audio logs and lore if you're into that), and then Lara's Nightmare takes the Croft Manor map and makes a whole Resident Evil and COD zombie like gamemode which is actually really good.

TL;DR, pretty good semi-linear game with a huge focus on exploration (more so than something like Uncharted) and decent combat that's worth checking out.

the gameplay feels more unsolid than tomb raider 2013, the 70% of world looks sucks, the music not good. the story SUCKS, its pathetic, naiv. magic fantasy, childish, unlogical most and realistic and gets more bad constantly to the end. the whole game feels same just with different areas. after maybe 70% of gameplay i didnt had fun much at all, it senseless time wasting what i felt i experienced...
 : ( but for the price from steam sales of 10.50 for all 3 games i cant complain. and so i cant not to recommend.

Rise of the Tomb Raider is one of those games that perfectly balances action, exploration, and storytelling. Lara Croft's journey feels more personal and mature, and watching her grow into the iconic adventurer is genuinely engaging.

The environments are beautiful, from frozen mountains to ancient tombs packed with puzzles and hidden secrets. Exploration is rewarding, combat is satisfying, and the survival mechanics add just enough depth without becoming tedious.

While the story follows a familiar treasure-hunting formula, the strong presentation and memorable locations keep things interesting from start to finish. It's not just about shooting enemies—it's about discovering lost places and uncovering mysteries.

If you enjoy cinematic action-adventure games with great exploration and a strong protagonist, Rise of the Tomb Raider is absolutely worth playing.

A far better Tomb Raider game than TR 2013. 

Although not as good as originals. Raiding tombs is still an optional objective. 
However, tombs here are bigger, prettier and completing them rewards perks. Puzzles are very easy. Lots of vertical traversal. Overall plays closer to original Tomb Raider core.

There are far less shootouts here, exploration is now a primary goal. Which is what Tomb Raider is supposed to be. Not a generic 3rd person shooter, where you eliminate hundreds of human enemies, like 2013 version is. The 2013 version is a good game but a horrible Tomb Raider game.

Not good as originals, but overall a good game and a decent Tomb Raider game.

Beats Shadow of Tomb Raider in terms of puzzles and gameplay. The last act could have been better imo but it doesn't take away from the whole game. Crafting/Upgrades are actually meaningful. Story moves a bit fast but you're able to keep up. Environments are actually really nice and hold up to today. Highly recommend to anyone looking for a fun Tomb Raider game.
Beautiful world, great story, and satisfying gameplay. Easily one of Lara Croft's best adventures. 9/10

Moderately better than Tomb Raider (2013), with slightly more challenging gameplay and more variation even on the easiest difficulty. It's great if you're already a fan, but I decided to play only the main story. There are two optional story DLCs, I didn't bother trying Baba Yaga, but Blood Ties was better than I expected despite the lack of action.
Great Game. High action controller game, that you can't stop playing...

the enviroment is absolutely stunning, cant tell you how many times i stopped just to look around. the snow on lara and in the worls looks so nice and the mountains are breathtaking. Story is really simple but cool enough to get you hooked. Sometimes the controls can be janked up but not so bad that your annoyed. Overall really visually stunning game.
pretty good game, gameplay is very fun, story is actually good and interesting with emotional characters, and the plot twists make it even better holds up really well for a 2015 game

85%
+ Improved animations and combat/traversal mechanics
+ Beautiful map/environment
+ Solid/Interesting Story
+ Fluid, reasonably challenging gameplay
- Large scale fights seem out of place
- Final missions sour the ending
- Additional traversal mechanics seem unnecessary
- Crafting availability dependent on environment/enemies faced

I prefer the story in Tomb Raider (2013) but Rise of the Tomb Raider is so fun to play, and looks absolutely gorgeous! Really enjoyed unlocking all the skills, finding the collectables, and exploring the tombs. Great game :)

I remember being surprised by how much I loved the first Tomb Raider (2013) reboot with it's immersive and gorgeous environments, satisfying skill trees and upgrades, great voice acting and large scale of content, this is a perfect run up to that without feeling like a blueprint rehash.
Amazing game. It surpassed the 2013 Tomb Raider in almost every way for me. Lara feels much more confident as a tomb raider. Highly recommended. 👍
loved every second of this game including the impossible missions that required months to finish.

It’s my favorite entry in the series. Although it may contain fewer adventurous moments, it excels in delivering thrilling action and immersive exploration, all while further developing Lara Croft’s captivating story 😍❤️

Actually, this is my second playthrough, and the beauty of the environments still holds up against many modern games.

✅ Pros:
Beautiful environments that make exploration genuinely rewarding.
The optional tombs contain some of the best puzzle content in the game.
I love the versatility of Lara's bow and its various functions throughout the adventure.

❌ Cons:
I'm not a fan of the fast travel system requiring you to return to a camp before traveling elsewhere.

🧠 Thoughts:
Rise of the Tomb Raider strikes a great balance between action and exploration. The bow is incredibly satisfying to use and, at times, can make combat feel a little too easy. Character progression feels rewarding, the outfit selection is excellent, and the puzzles are engaging without becoming frustrating.

The environments are still one of the game's greatest strengths. Whether you're exploring frozen mountain peaks, dense forests, or villages, every area is stunning and memorable. Even on a second playthrough, I found myself stopping just to admire the scenery.

And... I love Lara. She's a charismatic protagonist, and her design in this game is absolutely gorgeous.
I finally got aorund to finishing this game after having it in my library for ages. Loved it like the first, the only thing I can complain on is outdated controls, but that's just how it is.
100% a good game. Runs well unsurprisingly, and great story.
Its a good tomb raider game. Not much else to say, if you like action platformers you will like this.
Its Such A Good Game, I havent FInsihed It Yet But So Far Its So Fun , Somtimes it can be comfusing!!
The game feels kind of sloppy. There are a lot of gimmicks that don't really contribute to the game.

The environments are beautiful, the tombs and puzzles are fun to explore, and the mix of action, platforming, and exploration feels well balanced. Lara's character development and the overall story kept me interested throughout the journey. The game looks great even years after release.
Great game, scratches the same itch as Uncharted except it has the GOAT Lara Croft. Very excited for Legacy of Atlantis.

First Tomb Raider i've ever beaten and it was such a great game!

The sequel to Tomb raider (2013), it follows Lara's adventures before she becomes Tomb Raider and the game has a great setting, great story and these games are just as good as the classics! 

Due to it being a modern Tomb Raider game this is much more easily accessible for most players than the classics while still giving such great gameplay and story, DEFINITELY worth playing, 10/10!

Enjoyability - 7/10
Soundtrack - 7/10
Mod Content - 4/10
Difficulty - 5/10
Story - 6/10

It's the first game while being better at everything but the plot.

I actually am really liking it, I was hesitant at first because normally I don't like games like the tomb raiders. But i have to say this has been one of my favorite play through in a while and I'm still enjoying it. I'm hoping to complete it with 100% so still take a minute because I'm 15 hours in.
Some Puzzles are extremly frustrating otherwise good gameplay and amazing locations

[h2]A Solid, Beautiful Sequel (With Some Narrative Growing Pains)[/h2]
I jumped into this right after finishing the 2013 reboot. While it expands beautifully on the gameplay, mechanics, and visuals of the first game, the story pacing and character writing felt like a bit of a step back for a large chunk of the ride.

[b]Story & Pacing: A Bit of a Tantrum[/b]
[list]
[*]The Lara Problem: For about three-quarters of the game, Lara’s single-minded obsession with finding "that" artifact felt less like a driven archaeologist and more like listening to a child throwing a tantrum because she can't have a specific toy. Hahaha.
[*]Late Redemption: Thankfully, the final quarter of the game serves as a great redemption arc that brings it all together beautifully.
[*]Pacing Peaks & Valleys: The intro is incredibly solid and hooks you instantly. However, the middle section starts to feel dragging to the point where I found myself asking, "When is this actually going to end?"
[*]Keep it Linear: On the bright side, it sticks to the franchise's formula of keeping sidequests to a minimum. If you're just here for the main story, you can focus on it entirely without getting sidetracked.
[/list]

[b]Gameplay & Terrain Puzzles (The Highlight)[/b]
[list]
[*]Brilliant Environmental Puzzles: The terrain puzzles are absolutely fantastic. It’s rare nowadays to play a game where you never feel so completely trapped that you have to look up a guide on the internet just to figure out what to do next. Everything felt incredibly intuitive.

[i](Shoutout to Darksiders 2 for training my brain for this over the years, hahaha)[/i]
[/list]

[b]Visuals & Performance[/b]
[list]
[*]Aged Beautifully: Just like the first game, the graphics are phenomenal for the era it was released in. The snowy environments, lighting, and facial animations still look great today.
[/list]
first ever single player game I have played I would recommend such a good game for the price
I never did get into Tomb Radier games much, but after seeing some guys play this and the Indiana Jones - action packed game is actually really fun and interesting. I was missing out.

This is a fantastic follow-up to the first game in the series! If you`re looking for cinematic, pure adventure, beautiful visuals, and stories that constantly pull you forward, this is absolutely that game.

Upgrades, outfits, unlockable weapons, relics, and documents all feel rewarding to collect through exploration, and it makes the whole experience feel even more wholesome and immersive. I`m 80% through the game after finishing the main campaign, and I`ve really enjoyed the puzzles, hunting collectables, experimenting for achievements, and exploring the landscapes... and there's still more!

About DLCs: Croft Manor is a short but very fun dive if you`re in the lore. And the Baba Yaga DLC is intense, atmospheric, and incredibly memorable--I absolutely loved that one.

It also ran beautifully on the Steam Deck OLED! High settings at 45FPS, and battery life hit around 2.5hrs.

I can`t wait to jump into Shadow of the Tomb Raider next!
Fun game found a couple of bugs nothing game breaking yet

Just like the first game in the series, but more streamlined. Though, dragging a lot longer than it should've. Graphics are the same, but the game now is more demanding. All equipment/gear is given very late, which is a shame. Gameplay is good, Lara is beautiful, but the plot is meh, like always. Overall, I recommend it if you like to explore locations, solve puzzles and shoot stuff.

This is the 2nd best Tomb raider game I have ever experienced. Quality of graphics, atmosphere, stealth which Im not good for now, is so enjoyful and stressful at the same time. Didnt finish this game yet, but im looking forward to it. After the 2013, this is my 2nd best like I told. I still have 2006, 2007 and Remastered I-III from Epic store which is for free( i dont know if its still free). They may change my mind. Anyway good storytelling and game in general
this games story and ending was just so good my only complaint was i think the fighting mechanics were better in the 2013 game
A game that is pretty complete and polished for its core gameplay. They story is good (storytelling coherent ) but nothing amazing either.

I'm trying out gaming after a long time and since I don't have a PC right now to use Linux and play more games, I bought this since it was on sale and is mac compatible. Since macbook air doesn't have a fan its better to play at 1080p with low settings for a smoother gameplay. Its playable.

Loved the game so far, would buy part 1 and part 3 of this game after completing this. Another decision to make is to either get a PC, XBOX or Playstation.
it's really a good game, nothin else i wanna say, just this game is worth to buy and play, it has a rich game story, but as you know every lara croft games mostly she's always working alone

i just played 3 hours into the game and all i can say rn is, this game is a masterpiece !!

edit after finishing the story : this game is more than masterpiece !!

Really nice game, highly recommended ! Best Experience with Controller, Amazing Graphics, One may find boring if you are trying to complete whole game finding each and everything, repeated actions might feel frustating, else it's story is really good.

My favorite of the tomb raider survival trilogy I love the icy setting the climate mechanic is cool and the gun play is fun Lara Croft is a badass every lara Croft game is amazing yes even I'm just going to say Laura croft 6 Angel of darkness Lara Croft is an amazing character that deserves more recognition best female protagonist one of the best I love her games and I truly think she's a badass

Pretty much every single element in this sequel represents a massive, undeniable upgrade from the 2013 original. Visually, the graphics are absolutely stunning, while the platforming and climbing mechanics have received a major, much-needed level-up in fluidity. My primary critique of the first game was its disappointing lack of actual tombs and puzzles, so I was incredibly thrilled to find an abundance of them here—and they are significantly more creative and rewarding to solve.
10/10 game would recommend the puzzles are nice the game mechanics are fun and its runnable on mid range laptop

This may actually be better than the 2013 TR.  Way better mechanics.  However the game does mess up in climbing. I kept falling off because this woman doesnt know how to jump properly.  it may just be a skill issue tho

Rise of the Tomb Raider is an amazing adventure game with a great story, beautiful graphics, and fun exploration. Lara Croft's character development is excellent, and the tomb puzzles are both challenging and rewarding. The combat feels smooth, and the environments are stunning to explore. Even after several hours, the game keeps introducing new mechanics and interesting locations. If you enjoy action-adventure games with exploration, survival elements, and a strong story, this game is definitely worth playing.
very good story. graphics are nice. and the free exploring is also very delightfull.

Only issue I have had is the Black Texture glitch when using Tesselation and DX12 together (so I turned of Tesselation). In 2026, getting this game and its siblings on huge discounts and playing at good framerate on modern hardware feels so nice. The dialog can be weird at times, but I guess that tracks with movie style writing.

This game is absolute beautiful in every way. the world building, story, and graphics are absolutely beautiful. The game feels way ahead of its time and even today it still looks better than a lot of modern games. The atmosphere and exploration make the experience incredibly immersive.
fun and engaging gameplay ran also well at max settings
very good game! so much action and different avenues of gameplay. would 10/10 recommend
having fun. can be played without the previous part but it is also good so play that too

I have recently finished tomb raider 2013 and immediately started rise of tomb raider and so far it is so promising, visuals are awesome, lara as always gorgous af, puzzles are interesting and engaging.

The best thing about tomb raider games is the gameplay and stealth mecanism which is so satisfying.
This game still holds up incredibly well with its perfect balance of story, exploration, and action.
this is a fantastic game. it runs well on a M2 macbook. i highly suggest this game. also story is great.

always struggled with the first 10% of these games, but the midgame is so good it's worth it. Sorry I slept on this for so many years, significant improvement over the first one which I already enjoyed.

Some 'of the time' videogamey things (I still get the 'oh no I went the right way' feeling.

There is so much content. I like having alternate modes outside of the main story and with the DLC there is so much to do (Expedition modes).

Some of the legacy square enix account stuff is annoying.
Pure art: storytelling, physics, and game mechanics are incredible. Rise of the Tomb Raider is an incredible sequel in the Tomb Raider Survivor trilogy. This is an absolute must play!
Great second game of the trilogy, improves all aspects.

I love this series of games for their challenging quests.  No, this game does not treat you as if you were an idiot. Highly recommended. 
P.S.: Bots are stupid, yes, but then again I played on normal level.

It's a decent small scale openish world game. The gameplay is pretty good fun and it's easy to recommend as it's very cheap on sale.

However I will criticise it for it's boring completely forgettable story and terrible over the top cutscenes. Almost every scene something is exploding or whatever Lara was walking/climbing is collapsing. When there is so much mindless action, that none of it manages to be exciting or interesting. In just a few cutscenes at the start Lara takes so much abuse that even Spider-Man would end up in the hospital.

A fun story game with a good story and great graphics, while having low system requirements and is often heavily discounted, making it worth the money.
Definitly recommended!
came back after years expecting nostalgia… ended up getting addicted all over again, the vibe still hits hard just like the first time I've played it. one of my favorite game series ever.
The game is amazing in every aspect! story, gameplay, mechanics, graphics, music and puzzles, I can't recommend it enough, it's one of the greatest games ever.

fun, some improvements from TR(2013) i rly like. the bow n arrow has lots more options, graphicially also very improved from previous games. the only negative i have is that it can feel a little bloated with the "open world". other than that had a great time

Rise of the Tomb Raider is a masterpiece of the action-adventure genre. If you want a game with incredible graphics, satisfying gunplay, great stealth, and genuinely fun puzzles to solve, this is the best entry in the modern trilogy to play. i will give it a 10/10 of course!
Awesome game to play, this game have briliant graphic for 2013 year game. I bought it for around ~2 dollars and it is deserve this. Wating for sale to buy next espisode.

The second is the best, the third is good, and the first is best skipped.

Each game in the series has two problems:
- They're too long and have too many false endings—the scourge of the era.
- There's no acrobatics like in the very first games.

Does not need to play the first Tomb raider game (2013)  to play this one. 

Only one character is in this game from the one in 2013 but it's not important to the story. Story is okay and takes like 10 hours to play through. There is alot of improvements with combat in this compared to the first game, with alot of crafting.
If you enjoy exploration, puzzle-solving, and cinematic adventure, Rise of the Tomb Raider delivers one of the most polished experiences in the franchise.
fantastic game, graphics are exquisite, gameplay is more engaging, DLCs are fun tho the available mods are very spicy

I love the challenges this game brings and side quests. I also love being able to interact with the other bot characters either for quest or just small talks. I don't have any complains for this game.

i love games like tomb raider. The cinematic action, buildings falling apart Are just complete cinema for me. I had played the first part and that was great but this one is better, as It feels More adventurous And Lara Croft now looks more confident. Which is great as in the first part I moreover noticed that the game feels more adventure plus survival. but this one for me this is An cinematic experience.

I honestly feel like people sleep on this game series sometimes. Thank you Eidos for the bangers. 
It's not reinventing the wheel, probably won't change your life, but it is a solid, well made, good game.

Nice story, nice graphics, runs well, I like the systems involving upgrading gear, lots of stuff to do in the maps, Lara is a cutie, what else do you need?
final score: I really like this game/10

Also it goes often on sale, totally 100% worth it.

Rise of the Tomb Raider, is in my opinion a great follow up to the 2013 title, it improves on just about every detail, there's more customisation, from more weapons variety, to different types of arrows and other ammunition, to even being able traverse in new and fun ways, the combat feels way more refined, not to mention how stunning this game is to look at. Also this time out they added more actual tombs to explore, the only really draw back was the story, don't get me wrong I still enjoyed it but it is still just okay, It’s a pretty generic 'stop the evil organization' plot, and the villains are so generic, thankfully the core of the game is enjoyable. 

Overall it worth the time it takes to playthrough and I'd definitely recommend checking this game out, especially if you we're a fan on the previous instalment 
Solid 8/10

Good game, 8.5/10. I believe this is the best combat experience compared to the other survivor trilogy. Even better, it's surpassed in every aspects. People should buy this game rather than the whole bundle; playable as a standalone game even though it's sequel. Better story, better combat, better exploring/looting, better parkour, and better atmosphere compared to the 1st & 3rd game (Which is too lame/decent).
It may be a decade old game with some clumsy parkour, but it's still a lot of fun! One of the best on-the-rails story action games out there!

Compared to Tomb Raider (2013):

Things that got better: 

Graphics/Environments, Weapon & Gear Varietyc

Things that got worse: 

Lara Croft character model (they seem to have intentionally made her less attractive compared to 2013 and prior entries which is frankly a bummer because her sex appeal is half of the fun), Story/Writing, Character Development, Antagonist, Hand holding (the game is far too easy on PC even at the highest difficulties). 

Overall this is worth buying at a discounted price, but is not as good as the 2013 release.

I think this game is agreat example of making a squeal that carries over nearly every element from the first game, but adds slight tweeks to everything to make it all an even more fuffilling expirience.

I don't really have much to say on this game compared to 2013, but I really enjoyed it 

(exept for you baba yaga DLC puzzles you stupid fu-) 

9/10

I for some reason decided to start with the second game in the Tomb Raider Survivor trilogy for my first foray into the whole Tomb Raider franchise, which worked out pretty well somehow. While being my first TR game it was also my first time playing something with more intense gun based combat (closet I have played to that previously was Ratchet & Clank: Rift Part). I really enjoyed exploring and climbing around the expansive and secret filled environments and finding tombs tucked away. I found the story to be interesting enough to make an effort to continue looking for relics and documents for more story tidbits. I highly recommend Rise of the Tomb Raider if you enjoyed Tomb Raider (2013) as I feel it has a lot of the same strengths but with more depth mechanically (especially combat wise). 8/10

The story is beat for beat the same as the previous entry, with fewer characters. The writing's weak and the voice acting's sometimes comically bad. (Sorry Jacob.) The setting and tombs are all very generic. Unlike [i]Assassin's Creed[/i], I got no sense of being anywhere specific nor exploring any cultures. It was just snowier than the last game. All the lore was make believe. I've been spoiled by the [i]Arkham[/i] series, but very minor gameplay and technical improvements over the last one. Less zip line jank.

But none of that matters, because we're here for lady [i]Indiana Jones[/i]. The action set pieces are bigger and better and they beat up on Lara less gratuitously. The puzzles really impressed me. Not particularly difficult but fun to think though. I had a good time.

The DLC was a real grab bag. There was a walking simulator—the mansion seemed more like a big house than what I remember from the original, [i]original[/i] sequel. Then there was a bad boomer shooter using those same assets. There was a bare bones, multiplayer survival mode which was fun. The score attack, in which you replay segments of the game as both a speed run and a collectable pickup combo. Two very different types of OCD placation. And the meaty Baba Yaga one, which was integrated into the main game. Good but tonally different. Scarecrow side quest, basically. They were tryin' stuff.
Great game, I just finished the main story and the DLC and was a good 14 hr

Improves upon its predecessor in pretty much every way.

The combat feels mostly the same, but there is more weapon variety. The story is more intriguing and the characters feel more alive — still miles away from what it could be, but it’s good and engaging enough to keep you going.

My favorite part is the exploration and all the side content in the game. The platforming is a lot of fun too, and you keep unlocking new gear that helps you traverse the world. Basically, if you played the last game and liked it, you’re gonna love this one. If you played the last one and didn’t like it, well, I don’t think this is going to change your mind, because it’s mostly the same, just improved.

I tried the Croft Manor DLC and could not get through it. There’s basically no gameplay, only story and character bits, and I just found it boring.

I did not try the Expeditions mode though, but if you like the combat, I assume you would enjoy it.
I finished the game years ago and the Story is REALLY good the gameplay is nice and the characters are well thought out.

Finished the game at the current hours (39) with almost 100% done minus two bonus challenges that wer more tricky then i felt worth my time. 
Otherwise this is honestly were the current triology peaked. Tomb Raider was very good story wise and gameplay wise when i first played it, this one I really enjoyed the story and the gameplay wise was alright. 
None of the puzzles where too hard to solve, I didn't get stuck on any which was very nice as sometimes puzzles in games like this can bring the rating a bit down. My main complaints are in some more of the action scenes and running in which Lara wont grab onto things that she hypothetically should be able to which can make doing some areas really frustrating. 
All together i think if you enjoyed the first of this recent trilogy you'd probably like this one as well

Somehow this game started way better than the first one, but actually becomes tiring and self-repetative towards the end. Final boss is a shame to all helicopter bosses, and the bit where Kostyan takes our bow and we have to, for whatever reason, hit him only from behind is ridiculous. 
Characters are good, but the plot is somehow not that intriguing. 
Graphics are nice, but framerate still drops to 60 on open locations in 2026, bruh
Overall, i think this game should have been more linear, even more linear than the first one, and focused on good spectacle. All three major locations, except maybe for the last one, are extremely generic

Note that this review does NOT reflect the fine work of the world builders, animators, character modelers and voice actors .... they did a great job.  This unhappy critique of ROTR reflects the incompetence of the management and decision makers.

This game was great at the beginning and the tombs were great, but as you play the game the developers get more and more abusive and by the time you reach the flame thrower guy, you may ask yourself why you would put up with this kind of abuse on your own time.

The very best way to defeat the flame thrower guy .... is to delete the game, and never ever play a game by the video game studio Crystal Dynamics and its sub devs again. Having a lightly armoured guy that you can shoot in the face with a shotgun, or pistol, or rifle 50 times and do little damage is literally crapping in the players face. Its like a different group made the second half of the game. And yes shooting the tank is the obvious solution, but the devs deliberately increased the challenge of that with cheap cheap camera tricks. Simple truth for me ... I don not play games from devs I do not trust, and I no longer trust Crystal Dynamics (unless I can override them with modding or a modding community).  

Then there is the Baba Yaga DLC which has beautiful design, but a basically inflicts the player with 40 or so minutes of a Slavic woman screaming curses at you while you solve the same tedious puzzle over and over, and then she turns out to be a sweet old lady and you get a sanctimonious sermon about survival. 

Don't waste your time, this game would have been great if it was just Laura solving the tombs, and experiencing the beautiful world the worldbuilders made.

If this game had a significant modding community, the cheap camera tricks could be modded out, and this enemy could die when you shoot him 20 times point blank in the face with a shotgun or rifle, but of course even years after release, Crystal Dynamics won't have any of that modding action.  This is why I rarely play a game I cannot completely mod myself, and this game only cemented that.

We have Uncharted at home. 

As can be expected of a game with a budget of $100 million, Rise of the Tomb Raider looks and sounds great. It is also mostly functional. Sadly, in all other aspects of game design, it's mediocre at best. There's a plethora of half-baked game mechanics that are neither useful nor fun. For example, you get experience points that you can use to develop new skills, many of which only make you gain more XP. Not exactly an addictive gameplay loop. 

Although the scripted set pieces occasionally look epic, this game built upon jumping and climbing doesn't even have a proper physics engine. Sometimes Lara magically floats through the air to grab a ledge she shouldn't be able to reach, but if you try to defy the devs by making her traverse in a too creative way, she may fall through solid objects. 

Uninspired features or repetitive gameplay can be often overlooked in a cinematic game, if the story has you hooked. The story here manages to be pretentious, boring and ridiculous at the same time. Adding insult to injury, somebody thought it would be a great idea to spice the childishly stupid plot with some utterly inane anti-communist propaganda. Here's a free hint for aspiring video game writers: if you can't even make your characters talk like real human beings, please don't try to comment on political ideologies you've done zero research on. 

On the other hand, Rise of the Tomb Raider keeps spamming the player with onscreen tutorial messages right up to the underwhelming final boss fight. So It's probably not meant for people who like to think hard about things. Press X to raid a tomb.

As my first-ever Tomb Raider game, I feel heavily disappointed. What I can't deny is the beauty of the environment—it looks gorgeous—but apart from that...

The game is basically just: go there, find that, go there, find this now, BUT DO IT EXACTLY AS I SAY. You wanted to go through a path that looks like you could actually go there? Well, I'm sorry, you can't. Then why make it look like I can go there in the first place?

The movement in this game is something I simply hate. It feels sloppy, it feels weird, and what annoyed me the most was when you're climbing those ice walls and want to jump a little sooner than Lara does during her slow-ass animation. Well, sorry, you're going to die because you simply missed the jump.

The most disappointing thing in the game was the boss fight. And again, it was: do this, kill that, BUT ONLY THE WAY I TELL YOU TO. I accidentally revealed myself to Konstantine and thought, "Alright, I couldn't do it stealthily, so I'll just kill him normally." I kept spamming F, trying to stab him to death, but guess what? He's freaking immortal because you didn't do it the way the game wanted you to.

If the game had just a little more freedom, or at least allowed different approaches to certain situations, I think I could give it a pass. But it doesn't.

⭐ Steam Review for: Rise of the Tomb Raider ⭐

⏱️ Playtime: 30.3 Hours

Lara’s journey feels even more intense here. I liked seeing her become more experienced and confident, but at the same time the game keeps putting her in situations where she’s constantly pushed to her limits. 🧗‍♀️🐺

I really liked the story in this one a lot. I liked how the idea of immortality is woven into the story. I liked how it’s not treated like something simple or purely exciting, but more like something that makes people make questionable choices and believe in things for different reasons. It gave the story a bit more depth. It’s not a super new concept, but I think the game still uses it in a way that feels meaningful here and not just about “living forever,” but about what that kind of power actually does to people and whether it should even exist in the first place. ❄️🧊🪓

And it was nice having someone new (Jacob) join the story who actually becomes an ally instead of just feeling like another side character. It made parts of the journey feel a bit less lonely. 🏕️

The DLCs were a big mix for me though.

With Baba Yaga: The Temple of the Witch  I struggled a LOT. It was way harder than I expected and had me stuck more than once. I did get through it, but it definitely tested my patience more than I liked. 🧙🏻‍♀️

On the other hand, I really enjoyed Blood Ties. That one was actually really fun in a different way slower, more puzzle-focused, and I liked going around looking for clues and trying to figure out the code. It felt more relaxed and satisfying instead of stressful. 🕵🏽‍♀️🔍

I also tried the Cold Darkness Awakened DLC it honestly felt like “Trauma Raider” for me… it genuinely stressed me out. The atmosphere felt really tense the whole time because you constantly feel surrounded and unsafe. The base game feels more grounded survival/adventure, while this DLC suddenly leans much more into horror and almost zombie-like survival. It honestly felt like stepping into a completely different game. I did really liked the small objective/puzzle moments in the DLC where you actually had to figure out what to do instead of just following markers. I liked that mix of stress and figuring things out on my own. 🧟‍♂️

Overall, I really liked the base game a lot, and even though the DLC's made me suffer a bit, the other one made up for it. I really liked the story and gameplay a lot, even if I still have a soft spot for the first game’s setting. The island in Tomb Raider (2013) just felt more unique and memorable to me. Then again… I'm bit of a fan of Japan-themed locations, so that probably plays into it too. 🏔️🇯🇵 

I can't wait to continue Lara’s story in Shadow of the tomb raider. 🏹

One of the worst endings in gaming history. It's all just the same as in 2013: you first sneak through the place, get spotted, then fight undead and invading guys, boss fight, credits. But worse. A puzzle mid-fight that ruins the pacing. Out of the pocket 180 of Clara's motivation. Illuminati order setup at the end. Boss fight that was even worse than in the previous game. If 2013 was mid, then this is a pile of uninspired garbage.
